# Coding Guidelines

<!-- Generated by /ai-context. Manual edits are overwritten on re-run. -->

## AS IS — Current state

### 1. File-scoped namespaces

All `.cs` files use C# 10+ file-scoped namespace declarations (`namespace CertGuardMini;` / `namespace CertGuardMini.Models;` / `namespace CertGuardMini.Services;`). No block-scoped namespaces anywhere in the codebase. Enforced by project default (`ImplicitUsings` enabled + `Nullable` enabled in `CertGuardMini.csproj`).

### 2. Nullable reference types enabled globally

`<Nullable>enable</Nullable>` in `CertGuardMini.csproj` line 6. All reference types are nullable-aware throughout: `X509Certificate2?`, `CertificateInfo?`, `ProxyService?`. Null checks use `is not null` pattern consistently (`CertBrokerService.cs` lines 13, 143, 152; `MainWindow.xaml.cs` lines 76, 86, 107).

### 3. Event-driven service-to-UI communication

Services expose `EventHandler<T>` events rather than returning values or using callbacks. `CertBrokerService` fires `StatusChanged` and `DomainBlocked`. `ProxyService` fires `Log`, `RequestBlocked`, `RequestAllowed`, `CertificateUsed`. `MainWindow.xaml.cs` subscribes and marshals to UI thread via `Dispatcher.Invoke()`. No MVVM bindings, no INotifyPropertyChanged.

### 4. IDisposable pattern on services

Both `CertBrokerService` and `ProxyService` implement `IDisposable`. `CertBrokerService.Dispose()` calls `Unload()` which disposes the `X509Certificate2` and clears state. `ProxyService.Dispose()` calls `Stop()` then `_proxyServer.Dispose()`. Both call `GC.SuppressFinalize(this)`. No finalizers defined.

### 5. Ephemeral key storage for certificate private keys

`CertBrokerService.LoadFromPfxBytes` loads the PFX with `X509KeyStorageFlags.EphemeralKeySet | X509KeyStorageFlags.Exportable` (line 44). This ensures the private key never touches disk — a core security requirement stated in the README and enforced in code. `LoadSimulatedCertificate` sets `_certificate = null` (no real cert loaded).
